question_text: A compressed spring is released and pushes a ball across a frictionless surface. Which energy stores are involved in this process?

options.label: B
options.text: Elastic potential energy converting to kinetic energy

options.label: C
options.text: Kinetic energy converting to thermal energy

options.label: D
options.text: Chemical energy converting to elastic energy

explanation: The spring starts with energy in an elastic potential energy store. As it is released, this energy is converted to kinetic energy, making the ball move. On a frictionless surface, there is no energy loss to thermal energy.
